headline: Yemeni children at the heart of Houthi war machine
subhead: Since the coup at the end of 2014, Yemeni rebels have forcibly recruited thousands of minors each year, sending them to the front lines to fight against the government and Saudi Arabia.

headline: After two diplomats were violently detained in Tehran, France expresses concern for its nationals in Iran
US unveils new tariffs on 60 partners as Trump rebuilds trade agenda
Bernard Arnault, generous patron of the arts and lover of tax breaks
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (5/6). France's richest man is also one of Europe's leading art collectors. Through the Fondation Louis Vuitton, he combines his passion for the arts with highly lucrative tax arrangements.

headline: Bruno Cotte, magistrate: 'Contrary to what Marco Rubio claims, the International Criminal Court is neither right-wing nor left-wing'
subhead: The US Secretary of State's campaign to dismantle the ICC marks an assault on international law that should prompt much stronger reactions from its founders, especially the Europeans, argues the former ICC chamber president for Le Monde.

headline: Chanel's XXL cruise ship in May 2018
subhead: 'Behind the scene' (2/6). Fashion houses often use runway shows as opportunities for particularly spectacular set designs. In May 2018, Karl Lagerfeld docked a life-size cruise ship under the glass dome of the Grand Palais in Paris for Chanel's cruise collection show.

headline: The mysterious horse-shaped geoglyph England has been maintaining for thousands of years
subhead: The Uffington White Horse is one of the best known geoglyphs in the UK. Drawn around 3,000 years ago on a hillside in Oxfordshire, its chalk outline is maintained each year by volunteers through a restoration campaign.

How Macron narrowly averted his environment minister's resignation
subhead: Monique Barbut, who said she intended to quit the government on Tuesday, July 21, after an agricultural law she contested was adopted, ended up staying on in her role on Wednesday. The twist added a colorful note to the political crisis.

headline: How social media bans for young users, now in effect in France, have spread worldwide
subhead: France has decided to ban those under 15 from major platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. The country is following a global trend that has gained momentum in recent years.

headline: How Bernard Arnault works to keep his media outlets in line
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (4/6). Since the 1990s, the CEO of LVMH has built a small media galaxy in print and radio, which he uses to convey his ideas and tastes, but also to promote his brands.
